Politics.headline: Trump administration asks court to restore punishments for law firms
Politics.detail: The Justice Department told a federal appeals court that judges who blocked executive orders sanctioning several law firms had infringed on the president's authority.

Politics.headline: Controversial top vaccine regulator to depart FDA
Politics.detail: A controversial top vaccine regulator is set to leave the FDA.

Politics.headline: Under threat, Iraqi Kurds resist pressure to join Iran war
Politics.detail: Iraqi Kurds are caught in a difficult position amidst the cross-border Iran War, facing pressure to open the border from Iranian Kurds and threats from Tehran's military.

Politics.headline: Man convicted in plot to assassinate Trump that was tied to Iran's paramilitary
Politics.detail: A Pakistani business owner accused of trying to hire hit men to kill a U.S. politician has been convicted in a trial that showcased allegations of Iran-backed plotting on American soil.

Politics.headline: Trump removes DHS Secretary Kristi Noem after controversial tenure
Politics.detail: President Donald Trump announced the decision on social media and said Noem would be replaced with Sen. Markwayne Mullin (R-Okla.).

Finance & Economy.headline: Iran war pushes oil price above $90, threatening rise in global inflation
Finance & Economy.detail: The escalating conflict in the Middle East has caused a surge in oil prices, potentially leading to increased inflation worldwide.

Finance & Economy.headline: US economy shed 92K jobs in February, well below expectations
Finance & Economy.detail: The latest jobs report reveals a significant decline in employment, raising concerns about the strength of the American economy.

Finance & Economy.headline: Americans tap retirement funds at record rates as mounting financial stress takes toll
Finance & Economy.detail: More Americans are withdrawing from their 401(k)s due to increasing financial difficulties, signaling economic strain on households.

Finance & Economy.headline: Markets begin to react as Trump's attack on Iran risks hitting American pocketbooks
Finance & Economy.detail: The escalating conflict and market reactions are posing a threat to the financial stability of American households.

Finance & Economy.headline: US Treasury secretary hints at lifting more Russian oil sanctions post India move
Finance & Economy.detail: The US Treasury is considering easing sanctions on Russian oil following a move by India, potentially impacting global energy markets.

Technology.headline: Sony WH-1000XM6 review: An audio heavyweight with a few caveats
Technology.detail: The Sony WH-1000XM6 headphones offer excellent audio quality but have a few drawbacks.

Technology.headline: Everything Apple announced this week: $599 MacBook Neo, M5 Pro, iPhone 17e, and more
Technology.detail: Apple has unveiled a range of new products this week, including the budget-friendly MacBook Neo, the powerful M5 Pro chip, and the latest iPhone 17e.

Technology.headline: The best antivirus software to protect your computer in 2026
Technology.detail: Here are the top antivirus software options available in 2026 to safeguard your computer from malware and other threats.

Technology.headline: Hackers Are Trying to Steal LastPass Vault Details With Fake Support Emails
Technology.detail: Cybercriminals are using phishing emails disguised as support communications to trick LastPass users into revealing their vault credentials.

Technology.headline: Starlink vs. Physics. Can Cell Towers in Space Reach Devices Indoors?
Technology.detail: A discussion on the challenges and possibilities of using satellite-based cell towers, like Starlink, to provide cellular service to devices indoors.

Science.headline: Rare Mutation Identified as Direct Cause of Liver Disease
Science.detail: Mayo Clinic researchers have discovered a rare mutation in the MET gene that directly causes metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease, disrupting the liver's ability to process fat and leading to inflammation and scarring.

Science.headline: Cell-Free Cartilage Scaffold Helps Rebuild Damaged Bone
Science.detail: Researchers in Sweden have engineered a cell-free cartilage scaffold that guides the body to rebuild damaged bone by preserving the structure and natural growth signals, acting as a blueprint for the body's own repair mechanisms.

Science.headline: Newly Identified Protein May Prevent Diabetic Blindness
Science.detail: A newly identified protein, LRG1, may hold the key to preventing diabetic blindness by constricting tiny retinal blood vessels and reducing oxygen supply, marking the earliest damage in diabetic retinopathy.

Science.headline: Gravity is Weird in Antarctica
Science.detail: Gravity varies across the planet, and Antarctica is one of the strangest places, where gravity is slightly weaker than expected; scientists have traced this 'gravity hole' to slow, deep movements of rock.

Science.headline: Scientists Shocked to Find Two Hidden Species for Every One We Know
Science.detail: Scientists may have been dramatically undercounting the number of vertebrate species on Earth.

Health.headline: 5 new polio cases reported in S. Afghanistan in 2025: WHO-Xinhua
Health.detail: Five new cases of polio were confirmed in southern Afghanistan over the past year, with the WHO reporting that health authorities are facing difficulties in eliminating the contagious disease. Afghan health authorities and international partners are conducting nationwide vaccination campaigns to protect children and curb the spread of the virus.

Health.headline: Mayo Clinic identifies rare gene mutation causing liver disease
Health.detail: Mayo Clinic researchers have identified a rare mutation in the MET gene that can directly cause metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease, disrupting the liver's ability to process fat, leading to inflammation.

Health.headline: Cell-free cartilage scaffold can guide body to rebuild damaged bone
Health.detail: Researchers in Sweden have engineered a cell-free cartilage scaffold that can guide the body to rebuild damaged bone. By removing the cells but preserving the structure and natural growth signals, the material acts as a blueprint for the body's regeneration.

Health.headline: New protein may hold key to preventing diabetic blindness
Health.detail: A newly identified protein may hold the key to preventing diabetic blindness.

Health.headline: WHO warns Gaza medical supplies critically low amid limited aid access
Health.detail: The World Health Organization (WHO) warned that medicine stocks in the Gaza Strip are extremely low amid limited access for medical supplies to the enclave, with some basic materials already depleted. Half of Gaza's 36 hospitals have remained closed since the start of the ceasefire, and the Rafah Crossing remains closed, suspending medical evacuation operations for approximately 18,000 people.

Sports.headline: Russell on Australian GP pole in Mercedes lockout as Verstappen crashes out
Sports.detail: George Russell secured pole position for the Australian Grand Prix, leading a Mercedes one-two after Max Verstappen crashed out during qualifying.

Sports.headline: Luka Doncic scores 44 points in 3 quarters, Lakers beat Pacers
Sports.detail: Luka Doncic dominated with 44 points in just three quarters as the Lakers defeated the Pacers.

Sports.headline: Tatum returns for Boston Celtics after 10 months out with torn achilles.
Sports.detail: Jayson Tatum made his return to the Boston Celtics lineup after being sidelined for 10 months due to a torn Achilles tendon.

Sports.headline: Sammy's Desperate "Day 6" Message As West Indies Remain Stranded In India
Sports.detail: Daren Sammy expresses his concern as the West Indies team remains stranded in India.

Sports.headline: Dale Steyn Threatens New Zealand With 'Chokers' Tag Ahead Of India Final
Sports.detail: Dale Steyn jokingly threatens to label New Zealand as 'chokers' if they fail to win the T20 World Cup final against India, after their history of coming up short in major finals.

Entertainment.headline: Shohei Ohtani lights up social media with viral grand slam at World Baseball Classic
Entertainment.detail: Ohtani hit a grand slam in the second inning of the World Baseball Classic game against Taiwan, jumpstarting Japan's victory. The grand slam and subsequent RBI single electrified the crowd.

Entertainment.headline: Rock & Roll Hall of Fame unveils "diverse list" of 2026 nominees
Entertainment.detail: The Rock & Roll Hall of Fame has announced its nominees for 2026, featuring a wide array of artists.

Entertainment.headline: Britney Spears DUI Arrest: Troubled Star's Team Exploring Treatment Plan
Entertainment.detail: Britney Spears was arrested on suspicion of DUI, and her team is reportedly exploring treatment options. This follows an incident in California where she was taken into police custody.

Entertainment.headline: Disney Hath Slain the Live-Action Robin Hood
Entertainment.detail: Disney has reportedly cancelled the live-action Robin Hood remake. The cancellation comes as a disappointment to fans of the classic tale.

Entertainment.headline: Cancelled TV Shows of 2026: Which Series Are Ending This Year?
Entertainment.detail: 'The Witcher' is among the high-profile shows entering their final season in 2026.

Crime & Justice.headline: Kerala Sexual Assault Case: Survivor Moves Supreme Court Against Bail To MLA Rahul Mamkoottathil
Crime & Justice.detail: A woman has approached the Supreme Court challenging a Kerala High Court order granting anticipatory bail to a Congress MLA Rahul Mamkoottathil accused of rape, coercion and forcing her to terminate a pregnancy, alleging that the High Court made unwarranted remarks questioning her conduct while deciding the bail plea. The survivor challenged the Kerala High Court's anticipatory bail order before the Supreme Court, alleging the court made prejudicial observations questioning her conduct.

Crime & Justice.headline: Cuba announces fifth death after shootout with Florida-tagged speedboat
Crime & Justice.detail: The government in Havana has claimed that the 10 people on board speedboat planned to 'unleash terrorism' in Cuba.

Crime & Justice.headline: Group of boys as young as 13 convicted for raping girls, 14 and 15, in separate attacks
Crime & Justice.detail: Three teenage boys have today been convicted of a knife-point rape and other serious sexual offences against two teenage girls in Hampshire.

Crime & Justice.headline: Ex-DGP Sreelekha Moves Kerala High Court To Quash FIR For Allegedly Disclosing POCSO Victims' Names On YouTube Channel
Crime & Justice.detail: Former Director General of Police (DGP) and Thiruvananthapuram Corporation Councillor Sreelekha R. has moved the Kerala High Court seeking to quash an FIR registered against her for allegedly disclosing the names of victims in three rape cases through her YouTube channel and blog. Sreelekha is arrayed as the sole accused in a crime registered by the Museum Police for the offences punishable under Section 72 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and for the offences under Section 23 of the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man convicted in plot to assassinate Trump that was tied to Iran's paramilitary
Crime & Justice.detail: A Pakistani business owner accused of trying to hire hit men to kill a U.S. politician has been convicted in a trial that showcased allegations of Iran-backed plotting on American soil.

Education.headline: UPSC CSE Result 2025: What Does Provisional Status For 348 Candidates Mean?
Education.detail: NDTV Education reports on the UPSC CSE 2025 results and explains the meaning of provisional status for 348 candidates. The article provides details on what this status entails for those candidates.

Education.headline: India Post GDS Result 2026 Out: First List Of Shortlisted Candidates Released
Education.detail: NDTV Education announces the release of the first list of shortlisted candidates for the India Post GDS Result 2026. The article provides information on how to check the result and what the next steps are for the shortlisted candidates.

Education.headline: Inspector Father To Salute Daughter As She Returns Home As IPS Officer
Education.detail: NDTV Education shares an inspiring story of a father, an inspector, who will salute his daughter as she returns home as an IPS officer. The article highlights the daughter's achievements and the father's pride.

Education.headline: Meet Rajeswari Suve, Trainee Deputy Collector Who Secured AIR 2 In UPSC CSE
Education.detail: NDTV Education features Rajeswari Suve, a trainee Deputy Collector who secured AIR 2 in the UPSC CSE examination. The article shares her journey, preparation strategies, and success story.

Education.headline: CBSE Class 10 Social Science Paper Analysis: Difficulty Varied Across Sets, Says Educator
Education.detail: NDTV Education provides an analysis of the CBSE Class 10 Social Science paper, noting that the difficulty level varied across different sets, according to an educator. The article offers insights into the exam and its challenges.

Lifestyle.headline: 'The night Vijay and I danced as one': Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda channel modern day royals for their sangeet
Lifestyle.detail: Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda showcased modern-day royalty vibes for their sangeet ceremony.

Lifestyle.headline: Janhvi Kapoor celebrates her 29th birthday in a vibrant hot pink saree
Lifestyle.detail: Janhvi Kapoor celebrated her 29th birthday in a pretty pink saree during a temple visit, receiving warm wishes from celebrities.

Lifestyle.headline: Allu Sirish wears unique 'shanku-chakram' mehendi at wedding with Nayanika Reddy
Lifestyle.detail: Allu Sirish paid tribute to Lord Vishnu by wearing a unique 'shanku-chakram' mehendi at his wedding with Nayanika Reddy.

Lifestyle.headline: McDonald's CEO roasted after his tiny first bite of new Big Arch burger goes viral
Lifestyle.detail: McDonald's CEO Chris Kempczinski's review of the Big Arch burger, featuring a small bite and referring to the food as a "product," sparked online mockery.

Lifestyle.headline: Family of 6 exposes 'insanely expensive' airport food with pizza order, igniting online backlash
Lifestyle.detail: An Oregon mom's airport pizza hack went viral with 13 million views, sparking a heated online debate about the high cost of airport food.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The U.S. mission-creep risk in Iran
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Jason Willick discusses the risk of endless war with Iran and argues for the U.S. to declare victory and withdraw, rather than getting further entangled in the conflict. He suggests that while replacing the current regime would be ideal, settling for a weakened Iran might be a more prudent choice.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Election fraud claims undermine democracy
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece in the Concord Monitor discusses how claims of election fraud, particularly those repeated by Trump, undermine democracy. It argues that these claims lack evidence and are used to distract from real issues.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Unclear war aims are a recipe for disaster in Iran
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Republic News editorial argues that the Trump administration's war aims in Iran are unclear and potentially disastrous. The editorial highlights the lack of a coherent justification for the war and draws parallels to the Iraq War.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Bringing an end to homelessness
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Royal Gazette discusses the issue of homelessness in Bermuda, calling it a national disgrace. The editorial emphasizes the need for innovative solutions, including government initiatives and private sector involvement, to address the housing shortage and reduce the cost of living.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Protect state climate act's funds for intended uses
Opinion/Editorial.detail: HeraldNet.com discusses the importance of using funds from the state's Climate Commitment Act for their intended purposes, such as investing in infrastructure and programs that limit losses from climate-related disasters. The editorial emphasizes Washington's responsibility to show other states the possibilities and benefits of putting a price on carbon.

World.headline: Hezbollah 'gives evacuation warning to Israelis' near Lebanese border
World.detail: Hezbollah has reportedly issued an evacuation warning to Israelis residing near the Lebanese border, escalating tensions in the region. The warning comes amid ongoing conflict and heightened security concerns.

World.headline: IDF says it has unleashed 26 waves of strikes on Beirut since start of operation
World.detail: The Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) have stated that they have conducted 26 waves of strikes on Beirut since the beginning of their operation, intensifying military actions in the area. The strikes are part of an ongoing conflict with targets in Lebanon.

World.headline: Drones and missiles intercepted over Saudi Arabian airspace, defence ministry reports
World.detail: The Saudi Arabian Ministry of Defense has reported the interception of drones and missiles over its airspace. This incident highlights ongoing security concerns and regional tensions.

World.headline: US downplays reports Russia gave Iran intel to help Tehran strike US assets
World.detail: The United States is downplaying reports that Russia provided Iran with intelligence to assist Tehran in striking U.S. assets, amidst ongoing tensions in the region. Officials suggest the information may not be as significant as initially portrayed.

World.headline: At least 41 killed in Israeli air attacks on Lebanon's Bekaa: Ministry
World.detail: At least 41 people have been reported dead following Israeli air attacks on the Bekaa region of Lebanon, according to the Ministry. The attacks have further escalated tensions in the area.

Zimbabwe.headline: Chibaya urges Chamisa allies to exit Timba platform
Zimbabwe.detail: A power struggle within Zimbabwe's opposition has emerged after a message attributed to Amos Chibaya urged allies to leave the Timba platform.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe taking steps to clear $23bn debt
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is reportedly taking steps to clear its $23 billion debt to multilateral institutions, engaging in talks to address the issue.

Zimbabwe.headline: Marondera council fined US$5K over pollution
Zimbabwe.detail: The Marondera council has been fined US$5,000 for pollution-related offenses.

Zimbabwe.headline: China sends 23rd medical team to Zimbabwe, strengthening decades-long health partnership
Zimbabwe.detail: China has dispatched its 23rd medical team to Zimbabwe, reinforcing the long-standing health collaboration between the two nations.

Zimbabwe.headline: Indigenous churches throw weight behind Constitution changes
Zimbabwe.detail: Indigenous churches in Zimbabwe are supporting proposed changes to the Constitution.
